sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 22, level 2.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: - Player moved RIGHT from (18,27) to (18,30) - moves 3 pixels per action - Orange X cross moves with player, collects orange dots (47→46, one collected) - Same mechanic as level 1: cross arms collect matching-color dots - Need to collect remaining orange dots, then switch to red/blue crosses via SPACE - Orange dots in gray squares: (39,9), (42,24), (57,9) - need to verify current positions
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 1 of about 42 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the full picture:
- Orange X dots: 46 total, forming the X arms (43 dots) + 3 target dots at (39, 9), (42, 24), (57, 9)
- The X arms are diagonal lines from center (18, 30):
  - Up-left: (7, 19) to (17, 29) - 11 dots
  - Up-right: (7, 41) to (17, 31) - 11 dots
  - Down-left: (19, 29) to (29, 19) - 11 dots
  - Down-right: (19, 31) to (29, 41) - 11 dots
  - Total arm dots: 44, but we see 43 arm dots + 3 target dots = 46
Wait, let me count the arm dots more carefully from the list:
Up-left arm: (7,19), (8,20), (9,21), (10,22), (11,23), (12,24), (13,25), (14,26), (15,27), (16,28), (17,29) = 11
Up-right arm: (7,41), (8,40), (9,39), (10,38), (11,37), (12,36), (13,35), (14,34), (15,33), (16,32), (17,31) = 11
Down-left arm: (19,29), (20,28), (21,27), (22,26), (23,25), (24,24), (25,23), (26,22), (27,21), (28,20), (29,19) = 11
Down-right arm: (19,31), (20,32), (21,33), (22,34), (23,35), (25,37), (26,38), (27,39), (28,40), (29,41) = 10
Wait, I see (24, 24) in the down-left arm and (25, 37) in the down-right arm. Let me recount:
Down-left: (19,29), (20,28), (21,27), (22,26), (23,25), (24,24), (25,23), (26,22), (27,21), (28,20), (29,19) = 11
Down-right: (19,31), (20,32), (21,33), (22,34), (23,35), (25,37), (26,38), (27,39), (28,40), (29,41) = 10
Hmm, there's a gap in the down-right arm. (24, 36) is missing. Wait, (12, 36) is in the up-right arm. Let me check if (24, 36) is in the list... I don't see it. So the down-right arm has a gap at (24, 36).
Actually wait, I see (25, 37) in the list but not (24, 36). This could be because (24, 36) was the position where an orange dot was collected! That would explain why we went from 47 to 46 orange dots.
So when the player moved RIGHT, the X moved from center (18, 27) to (18, 30). The down-right arm at the old center would have passed through (24, 36), and there was an orange dot there that got collected.
Wait, but the orange dots in gray squares are at (39, 9), (42, 24), (57, 9). None of these are at (24, 36). So the collected dot must have been one of the X arm dots that overlapped with something... Actually no, the X arms ARE the orange dots. So when the X moved, one of the arm dots from the old position overlapped with a target dot.
Hmm, actually, I think the X arms are 13 pixels long (like level 1). Let me check:
From center (18, 30):
- Up-left: 18-13=5 to 18-1=17, so rows 5-17. But the dots start at row 7. So the arm length is 18-7=11 pixels.
- Down-right: 18+1=19 to 18+13=31, so rows 19-31. The dots go to row 29, so arm length is 29-19+1=11 pixels.
So each arm is 11 pixels long (not counting the center). Total: 4*11 = 44 arm dots + 3 target dots = 47. After collecting one, 46.
Now, the target orange dots are at (39, 9), (42, 24), (57, 9). I need to position the X so that its arms pass through these dots.
For a diagonal X, the arms are:
- Up-left: (center_row - k, center_col - k) for k = 1 to 11
- Up-right: (center_row - k, center_col + k) for k = 1 to 11
- Down-left: (center_row + k, center_col - k) for k = 1 to 11
- Down-right: (center_row + k, center_col + k) for k = 1 to 11
For dot (39, 9):
- Need center_row - k = 39 and center_col - k = 9 (down-left arm): center_row - center_col = 39 - 9 = 30, and k = center_row - 39
  - Or center_row + k = 39 and center_col - k = 9 (down-left): center_row + center_col = 39 + 9 = 48, k = 39 - center_row
  - Actually, for down-left arm: row = center_row + k, col = center_col - k
  - So: 39 = center_row + k, 9 = center_col - k
  - Adding: 48 = center_row + center_col
  - k = 39 - center_row = center_col - 9
  - Need 1 <= k <= 11
  - So center_row + center_col = 48, and 28 <= center_row <= 38, 10 <= center_col <= 40
- For up-left arm: row = center_row - k, col = center_col - k
  - 39 = center_row - k, 9 = center_col - k
  - center_row - center_col = 30
  - k = center_row - 39 = center_col - 9
  - Need 1 <= k <= 11
  - So center_row - center_col = 30, 40 <= center_row <= 50, 10 <= center_col <= 20
- For down-right arm: row = center_row + k, col = center_col + k
  - 39 = center_row + k, 9 = center_col + k
  - center_row - center_col = 30
  - k = 39 - center_row = 9 - center_col
  - Need 1 <= k <= 11
  - So center_row - center_col = 30, 28 <= center_row <= 38, -2 <= center_col <= 8
- For up-right arm: row = center_row - k, col = center_col + k
  - 39 = center_row - k, 9 = center_col + k
  - center_row + center_col = 48
  - k = center_row - 39 = 9 - center_col
  - Need 1 <= k <= 11
  - So center_row + center_col = 48, 40 <= center_row <= 50, -2 <= center_col <= 8
For dot (42, 24):
- Down-left: 42 = center_row + k, 24 = center_col - k → center_row + center_col = 66, k = 42 - center_row = center_col - 24
  - 31 <= center_row <= 41, 25 <= center_col <= 35
- Up-left: 42 = center_row - k, 24 = center_col - k → center_row - center_col = 18, k = center_row - 42 = center_col - 24
  - 43 <= center_row <= 53, 25 <= center_col <= 35
- Down-right: 42 = center_row + k, 24 = center_col + k → center_row - center_col = 18, k = 42 - center_row = 24 - center_col
  - 31 <= center_row <= 41, 13 <= center_col <= 23
- Up-right: 42 = center_row - k, 24 = center_col + k → center_row + center_col = 66, k = center_row - 42 = 24 - center_col
  - 43 <= center_row <= 53, 13 <= center_col <= 23
For dot (57, 9):
- Down-left: 57 = center_row + k, 9 = center_col - k → center_row + center_col = 66, k = 57 - center_row = center_col - 9
  - 46 <= center_row <= 56, 10 <= center_col <= 20
- Up-left: 57 = center_row - k, 9 = center_col - k → center_row - center_col = 48, k = center_row - 57 = center_col - 9
  - 58 <= center_row <= 68, 10 <= center_col <= 20
- Down-right: 57 = center_row + k, 9 = center_col + k → center_row - center_col = 48, k = 57 - center_row = 9 - center_col
  - 46 <= center_row <= 56, -2 <= center_col <= 8
- Up-right: 57 = center_row - k, 9 = center_col + k → center_row + center_col = 66, k = center_row - 57 = 9 - center_col
  - 58 <= center_row <= 68, -2 <= center_col <= 8
Now, the player moves in steps of 3 pixels. Current position is (18, 30).
Valid positions are: (18 + 3*m, 30 + 3*n) for integers m, n.
Let me find positions that collect multiple dots:
For (39, 9) and (42, 24):
- (39, 9) down-left: center_row + center_col = 48
- (42, 24) down-left: center_row + center_col = 66
These are different sums, so can't collect both on down-left arm.
- (39, 9) down-left: center_row + center_col = 48
- (42, 24) down-right: center_row - center_col = 18
  - Adding: 2*center_row = 66, center_row = 33, center_col = 15
  - Check: 33 + 15 = 48 ✓, 33 - 15 = 18 ✓
  - k for (39, 9): k = 39 - 33 = 6, valid (1-11) ✓
  - k for (42, 24): k = 42 - 33 = 9, valid (1-11) ✓
  - Position (33, 15): is it reachable? 33 = 18 + 5*3, 15 = 30 - 5*3. So 5 DOWN + 5 LEFT = 10 actions.
- (39, 9) down-left: center_row + center_col = 48
- (42, 24) up-right: center_row + center_col = 66
Different sums, can't both be on same arm type.
Let me check (33, 15) for all 3 dots:
- (39, 9): down-left, k = 39-33 = 6 ✓
- (42, 24): down-right, k = 42-33 = 9 ✓
- (57, 9): down-left, k = 57-33 = 24, too big (max 11) ✗
  - Or down-right: center_row - center_col = 48, 33 - 15 = 18 ≠ 48 ✗
  - Or up-left: center_row - center_col = 48, same ✗
  - Or up-right: center_row + center_col = 66, 33 + 15 = 48 ≠ 66 ✗
So (33, 15) collects (39, 9) and (42, 24) but not (57, 9).
For (57, 9):
- Down-left: center_row + center_col = 66, 46 <= center_row <= 56
- Reachable from (18, 30): center_row = 18 + 3*m, center_col = 30 + 3*n
- center_row + center_col = 48 + 3*(m+n) = 66 → m+n = 6
- And 46 <= 18 + 3*m <= 56 → 28 <= 3*m <= 38 → 9.33 <= m <= 12.67 → m = 10, 11, 12
- If m = 10: n = -4, center = (48, -2) - col out of bounds
- If m = 11: n = -5, center = (51, -5) - col out of bounds
- If m = 12: n = -6, center = (54, -8) - col out of bounds
Hmm, those are out of bounds. Let me try other arm combinations.
For (57, 9) down-right: center_row - center_col = 48
- 18 + 3*m - (30 + 3*n) = 48 → 3*(m-n) = 60 → m-n = 20
- center_row = 18 + 3*m, need 46 <= center_row <= 56 → m = 10, 11, 12
- If m = 10: n = -10, center = (48, 0). Check: 48 - 0 = 48 ✓, k = 57-48 = 9 ✓
  - Position (48, 0): reachable! 10 DOWN + 10 LEFT = 20 actions from (18, 30)
Let me check (48, 0) for all 3 dots:
- (39, 9): 
  - Down-left: 48 + 0 = 48 ≠ 48... wait, center_row + center_col = 48 + 0 = 48. For (39, 9) down-left: center_row + center_col = 48 ✓
  - k = 39 - 48 = -9, negative, not valid
  - Up-left: center_row - center_col = 48, 48 - 0 = 48 ✓
  - k = 48 - 39 = 9, valid ✓. And col: 0 - 9 = -9 ≠ 9. Hmm.
Wait, I need to be more careful. For up-left arm: row = center_row - k, col = center_col - k
- (39, 9): 39 = 48 - k → k = 9, 9 = 0 - k → k = -9. Inconsistent! ✗
Let me redo this more carefully.
For a center at (cr, cc), the X arms cover:
- Up-left: (cr-k, cc-k) for k=1..11
- Up-right: (cr-k, cc+k) for k=1..11
- Down-left: (cr+k, cc-k) for k=1..11
- Down-right: (cr+k, cc+k) for k=1..11
For dot (39, 9) at center (48, 0):
- Up-left: 39 = 48-k, 9 = 0-k → k=9, k=-9. Inconsistent ✗
- Up-right: 39 = 48-k, 9 = 0+k → k=9, k=9. Consistent! k=9, valid ✓
- Down-left: 39 = 48+k, 9 = 0-k → k=-9, k=-9. Negative ✗
- Down-right: 39 = 48+k, 9 = 0+k → k=-9, k=9. Inconsistent ✗
So (39, 9) is on the up-right arm from (48, 0).
For dot (42, 24) at center (48, 0):
- Up-left: 42 = 48-k, 24 = 0-k → k=6, k=-24. Inconsistent ✗
- Up-right: 42 = 48-k, 24 = 0+k → k=6, k=24. Inconsistent ✗
- Down-left: 42 = 48+k, 24 = 0-k → k=-6, k=-24. Negative ✗
- Down-right: 42 = 48+k, 24 = 0+k → k=-6, k=24. Inconsistent ✗
So (42, 24) is NOT on any arm from (48, 0). ✗
For dot (57, 9) at center (48, 0):
- Down-right: 57 = 48+k, 9 = 0+k → k=9, k=9. Consistent! k=9, valid ✓
So (48, 0) collects (39, 9) and (57, 9) but not (42, 24).
Hmm, it seems hard to collect all 3 at once. Let me think about collecting them in 2 positions.
Option 1: Collect (39, 9) and (42, 24) at (33, 15), then (57, 9) separately.
Option 2: Collect (39, 9) and (57, 9) at (48, 0), then (42, 24) separately.
For (33, 15):
From (18, 30): 5 DOWN + 5 LEFT = 10 actions
Then to collect (57, 9): need to find a position that collects (57, 9)
- (57, 9) down-right from (48, 0): center (48, 0)
- From (33, 15): 5 DOWN + 5 LEFT = 10 actions
Total: 20 actions
For (48, 0):
From (18, 30): 10 DOWN + 10 LEFT = 20 actions
Then to collect (42, 24): need to find a position
- (42, 24) down-left: center_row + center_col = 66, 31 <= center_row <= 41
- Reachable: center_row = 18 + 3*m, center_col = 30 + 3*n, m+n = (66-48)/3 = 6
- center_row = 18 + 3*m, 31 <= 18+3m <= 41 → 13 <= 3m <= 23 → m = 5, 6, 7
- m=5: n=1, center = (33, 33). Check: 33+33=66 ✓, k=42-33=9 ✓
  - From (48, 0): 5 UP + 11 RIGHT... 33-48=-15, -15/3=-5. 33-0=33, 33/3=11. So 5 UP + 11 RIGHT = 16 actions.
  - Total: 20 + 16 = 36 actions. That's a lot.
Hmm, let me think more efficiently. Maybe I should collect all 3 orange dots in 2 stops.
Let me find positions that collect 2 dots each:
For (39, 9) and (42, 24):
- (39, 9) up-right: center_row + center_col = 48, center_row >= 40
- (42, 24) down-left: center_row + center_col = 66, center_row <= 41
- Different sums, can't be on same arm type.
- (39, 9) up-right: center_row + center_col = 48
- (42, 24) up-right: center_row + center_col = 66
- Different sums.
- (39, 9) up-right: center_row + center_col = 48
- (42, 24) down-right: center_row - center_col = 18
  - Solving: center_row = 33, center_col = 15. Already found this.
- (39, 9) down-left: center_row + center_col = 48
- (42, 24) up-left: center_row - center_col = 18
  - Solving: center_row = 33, center_col = 15. Same position!
So (33, 15) collects (39, 9) and (42, 24). Let me verify:
- (39, 9) at center (33, 15):
  - Down-left: 39 = 33+k, 9 = 15-k → k=6, k=6. ✓
- (42, 24) at center (33, 15):
  - Down-right: 42 = 33+k, 24 = 15+k → k=9, k=9. ✓
Now for (57, 9):
- (57, 9) down-left: center_row + center_col = 66
- Reachable positions: center_row = 18 + 3*m, center_col = 30 + 3*n
- 18 + 3*m + 30 + 3*n = 66 → m + n = 6
- k = 57 - center_row = 57 - (18 + 3*m) = 39 - 3*m
- Need 1 <= k <= 11: 1 <= 39 - 3*m <= 11 → 28 <= 3*m <= 38 → m = 10, 11, 12
- m=10: n=-4, center = (48, -12). Out of bounds.
- m=11: n=-5, center = (51, -15). Out of bounds.
- m=12: n=-6, center = (54, -18). Out of bounds.
- (57, 9) down-right: center_row - center_col = 48
- 18 + 3*m - (30 + 3*n) = 48 → m - n = 20
- k = 57 - center_row = 57 - (18 + 3*m) = 39 - 3*m
- Need 1 <= k <= 11: m = 10, 11, 12
- m=10: n=-10, center = (48, 0). k = 9. ✓
- m=11: n=-9, center = (51, 3). k = 6. ✓
- m=12: n=-8, center = (54, 6). k = 3. ✓
Let me check (51, 3) for all 3 dots:
- (39, 9) at (51, 3):
  - Up-right: 39 = 51-k, 9 = 3+k → k=12, k=6. Inconsistent ✗
  - Up-left: 39 = 51-k, 9 = 3-k → k=12, k=-6. Inconsistent ✗
  - Down-left: 39 = 51+k → k=-12. Negative ✗
  - Down-right: 39 = 51+k → k=-12. Negative ✗
So (51, 3) doesn't collect (39, 9).
What about (54, 6)?
- (39, 9) at (54, 6):
  - Up-right: 39 = 54-k, 9 = 6+k → k=15, k=3. Inconsistent ✗
  - Up-left: 39 = 54-k, 9 = 6-k → k=15, k=-3. Inconsistent ✗
  - Down-left/right: k negative. ✗
So (54, 6) doesn't collect (39, 9) either.
It seems like (57, 9) is hard to combine with the other dots. Let me try a different approach: collect (42, 24) and (57, 9) together.
For (42, 24) and (57, 9):
- (42, 24) down-left: center_row + center_col = 66
- (57, 9) down-left: center_row + center_col = 66
Same sum! So they can be on the same arm.
- k for (42, 24): k = 42 - center_row
- k for (57, 9): k = 57 - center_row
- Need both 1-11: 31 <= center_row <= 41 and 46 <= center_row <= 56
- Overlap: 46 <= center_row <= 46 → center_row = 46
- center_col = 66 - 46 = 20
- Check: (42, 24) down-left: k = 42-46 = -4. Negative! ✗
Hmm, the k values overlap but in opposite directions. Let me try different arms.
- (42, 24) up-right: center_row + center_col = 66, center_row >= 43
- (57, 9) down-left: center_row + center_col = 66, center_row <= 56
- Same sum, different arm types.
- k for (42, 24) up-right: k = center_row - 42
- k for (57, 9) down-left: k = 57 - center_row
- Need 1 <= center_row - 42 <= 11: 43 <= center_row <= 53
- Need 1 <= 57 - center_row <= 11: 46 <= center_row <= 56
- Overlap: 46 <= center_row <= 53
- center_row = 18 + 3*m: 46 <= 18+3m <= 53 → 28 <= 3m <= 35 → m = 10, 11
- m=10: center_row = 48, center_col = 66-48 = 18. k1 = 48-42 = 6 ✓, k2 = 57-48 = 9 ✓
- m=11: center_row = 51, center_col = 66-51 = 15. k1 = 51-42 = 9 ✓, k2 = 57-51 = 6 ✓
Let me check (48, 18) for all 3 dots:
- (39, 9) at (48, 18):
  - Up-right: 39 = 48-k, 9 = 18+k → k=9, k=-9. Inconsistent ✗
  - Up-left: 39 = 48-k, 9 = 18-k → k=9, k=9. ✓! k=9, valid ✓
- (42, 24) at (48, 18):
  - Up-right: 42 = 48-k, 24 = 18+k → k=6, k=6. ✓! k=6, valid ✓
- (57, 9) at (48, 18):
  - Down-left: 57 = 48+k, 9 = 18-k → k=9, k=9. ✓! k=9, valid ✓
(48, 18) collects ALL 3 orange dots! 
From (18, 30) to (48, 18):
- Row: 48 - 18 = 30, 30/3 = 10 DOWN
- Col: 18 - 30 = -12, -12/3 = 4 LEFT
Total: 14 actions

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model:
- Level 2: Player controls orange diagonal X cross, moves 3 pixels per action
- Player at (18, 30), orange X arms are 11 pixels long in 4 diagonal directions
- 3 orange target dots to collect: (39, 9), (42, 24), (57, 9)
- Found optimal position: (48, 18) collects ALL 3 orange dots simultaneously
  - (39, 9): up-left arm, k=9
  - (42, 24): up-right arm, k=6  
  - (57, 9): down-left arm, k=9
- Path: 10 DOWN + 4 LEFT = 14 actions from (18, 30)

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move to (48, 18) to collect all 3 orange dots
# From (18, 30): 10 DOWN + 4 LEFT = 14 actions
actions = ['DOWN']*10 + ['LEFT']*4
result = action(actions)
print("Result:", result)

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Find player
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"Player at: {n['boundary']}")
        break

# Count remaining orange dots
orange_1px = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'O' and n['pixels'] == 1]
print(f"Orange 1px dots remaining: {len(orange_1px)}")

# Check level completion
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
print(f"Score: {last_action_result.get('score')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 22, level 2.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: - Player moved RIGHT from (18,27) to (18,30) - moves 3 pixels per action - Orange X cross moves with player, collects orange dots (47→46, one collected) - Same mechanic as level 1: cross arms collect matching-color dots - Need to collect remaining orange dots, then switch to red/blue crosses via SPACE - Orange dots in gray squares: (39,9), (42,24), (57,9) - need to verify current positions
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 1 of about 42 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the full picture:
- Orange X dots: 46 total, forming the X arms (43 dots) + 3 target dots at (39, 9), (42, 24), (57, 9)
- The X arms are diagonal lines from center (18, 30):
  - Up-left: (7, 19) to (17, 29) - 11 dots
  - Up-right: (7, 41) to (17, 31) - 11 dots
  - Down-left: (19, 29) to (29, 19) - 11 dots
  - Down-right: (19, 31) to (29, 41) - 11 dots
  - Total arm dots: 44, but we see 43 arm dots + 3 target dots = 46
Wait, let me count the arm dots more carefully from the list:
Up-left arm: (7,19), (8,20), (9,21), (10,22), (11,23), (12,24), (13,25), (14,26), (15,27), (16,28), (17,29) = 11
Up-right arm: (7,41), (8,40), (9,39), (10,38), (11,37), (12,36), (13,35), (14,34), (15,33), (16,32), (17,31) = 11
Down-left arm: (19,29), (20,28), (21,27), (22,26), (23,25), (24,24), (25,23), (26,22), (27,21), (28,20), (29,19) = 11
Down-right arm: (19,31), (20,32), (21,33), (22,34), (23,35), (25,37), (26,38), (27,39), (28,40), (29,41) = 10
Wait, I see (24, 24) in the down-left arm and (25, 37) in the down-right arm. Let me recount:
Down-left: (19,29), (20,28), (21,27), (22,26), (23,25), (24,24), (25,23), (26,22), (27,21), (28,20), (29,19) = 11
Down-right: (19,31), (20,32), (21,33), (22,34), (23,35), (25,37), (26,38), (27,39), (28,40), (29,41) = 10
Hmm, there's a gap in the down-right arm. (24, 36) is missing. Wait, (12, 36) is in the up-right arm. Let me check if (24, 36) is in the list... I don't see it. So the down-right arm has a gap at (24, 36).
Actually wait, I see (25, 37) in the list but not (24, 36). This could be because (24, 36) was the position where an orange dot was collected! That would explain why we went from 47 to 46 orange dots.
So when the player moved RIGHT, the X moved from center (18, 27) to (18, 30). The down-right arm at the old center would have passed through (24, 36), and there was an orange dot there that got collected.
Wait, but the orange dots in gray squares are at (39, 9), (42, 24), (57, 9). None of these are at (24, 36). So the collected dot must have been one of the X arm dots that overlapped with something... Actually no, the X arms ARE the orange dots. So when the X moved, one of the arm dots from the old position overlapped with a target dot.
Hmm, actually, I think the X arms are 13 pixels long (like level 1). Let me check:
From center (18, 30):
- Up-left: 18-13=5 to 18-1=17, so rows 5-17. But the dots start at row 7. So the arm length is 18-7=11 pixels.
- Down-right: 18+1=19 to 18+13=31, so rows 19-31. The dots go to row 29, so arm length is 29-19+1=11 pixels.
So each arm is 11 pixels long (not counting the center). Total: 4*11 = 44 arm dots + 3 target dots = 47. After collecting one, 46.
Now, the target orange dots are at (39, 9), (42, 24), (57, 9). I need to position the X so that its arms pass through these dots.
For a diagonal X, the arms are:
- Up-left: (center_row - k, center_col - k) for k = 1 to 11
- Up-right: (center_row - k, center_col + k) for k = 1 to 11
- Down-left: (center_row + k, center_col - k) for k = 1 to 11
- Down-right: (center_row + k, center_col + k) for k = 1 to 11
For dot (39, 9):
- Need center_row - k = 39 and center_col - k = 9 (down-left arm): center_row - center_col = 39 - 9 = 30, and k = center_row - 39
  - Or center_row + k = 39 and center_col - k = 9 (down-left): center_row + center_col = 39 + 9 = 48, k = 39 - center_row
  - Actually, for down-left arm: row = center_row + k, col = center_col - k
  - So: 39 = center_row + k, 9 = center_col - k
  - Adding: 48 = center_row + center_col
  - k = 39 - center_row = center_col - 9
  - Need 1 <= k <= 11
  - So center_row + center_col = 48, and 28 <= center_row <= 38, 10 <= center_col <= 40
- For up-left arm: row = center_row - k, col = center_col - k
  - 39 = center_row - k, 9 = center_col - k
  - center_row - center_col = 30
  - k = center_row - 39 = center_col - 9
  - Need 1 <= k <= 11
  - So center_row - center_col = 30, 40 <= center_row <= 50, 10 <= center_col <= 20
- For down-right arm: row = center_row + k, col = center_col + k
  - 39 = center_row + k, 9 = center_col + k
  - center_row - center_col = 30
  - k = 39 - center_row = 9 - center_col
  - Need 1 <= k <= 11
  - So center_row - center_col = 30, 28 <= center_row <= 38, -2 <= center_col <= 8
- For up-right arm: row = center_row - k, col = center_col + k
  - 39 = center_row - k, 9 = center_col + k
  - center_row + center_col = 48
  - k = center_row - 39 = 9 - center_col
  - Need 1 <= k <= 11
  - So center_row + center_col = 48, 40 <= center_row <= 50, -2 <= center_col <= 8
For dot (42, 24):
- Down-left: 42 = center_row + k, 24 = center_col - k → center_row + center_col = 66, k = 42 - center_row = center_col - 24
  - 31 <= center_row <= 41, 25 <= center_col <= 35
- Up-left: 42 = center_row - k, 24 = center_col - k → center_row - center_col = 18, k = center_row - 42 = center_col - 24
  - 43 <= center_row <= 53, 25 <= center_col <= 35
- Down-right: 42 = center_row + k, 24 = center_col + k → center_row - center_col = 18, k = 42 - center_row = 24 - center_col
  - 31 <= center_row <= 41, 13 <= center_col <= 23
- Up-right: 42 = center_row - k, 24 = center_col + k → center_row + center_col = 66, k = center_row - 42 = 24 - center_col
  - 43 <= center_row <= 53, 13 <= center_col <= 23
For dot (57, 9):
- Down-left: 57 = center_row + k, 9 = center_col - k → center_row + center_col = 66, k = 57 - center_row = center_col - 9
  - 46 <= center_row <= 56, 10 <= center_col <= 20
- Up-left: 57 = center_row - k, 9 = center_col - k → center_row - center_col = 48, k = center_row - 57 = center_col - 9
  - 58 <= center_row <= 68, 10 <= center_col <= 20
- Down-right: 57 = center_row + k, 9 = center_col + k → center_row - center_col = 48, k = 57 - center_row = 9 - center_col
  - 46 <= center_row <= 56, -2 <= center_col <= 8
- Up-right: 57 = center_row - k, 9 = center_col + k → center_row + center_col = 66, k = center_row - 57 = 9 - center_col
  - 58 <= center_row <= 68, -2 <= center_col <= 8
Now, the player moves in steps of 3 pixels. Current position is (18, 30).
Valid positions are: (18 + 3*m, 30 + 3*n) for integers m, n.
Let me find positions that collect multiple dots:
For (39, 9) and (42, 24):
- (39, 9) down-left: center_row + center_col = 48
- (42, 24) down-left: center_row + center_col = 66
These are different sums, so can't collect both on down-left arm.
- (39, 9) down-left: center_row + center_col = 48
- (42, 24) down-right: center_row - center_col = 18
  - Adding: 2*center_row = 66, center_row = 33, center_col = 15
  - Check: 33 + 15 = 48 ✓, 33 - 15 = 18 ✓
  - k for (39, 9): k = 39 - 33 = 6, valid (1-11) ✓
  - k for (42, 24): k = 42 - 33 = 9, valid (1-11) ✓
  - Position (33, 15): is it reachable? 33 = 18 + 5*3, 15 = 30 - 5*3. So 5 DOWN + 5 LEFT = 10 actions.
- (39, 9) down-left: center_row + center_col = 48
- (42, 24) up-right: center_row + center_col = 66
Different sums, can't both be on same arm type.
Let me check (33, 15) for all 3 dots:
- (39, 9): down-left, k = 39-33 = 6 ✓
- (42, 24): down-right, k = 42-33 = 9 ✓
- (57, 9): down-left, k = 57-33 = 24, too big (max 11) ✗
  - Or down-right: center_row - center_col = 48, 33 - 15 = 18 ≠ 48 ✗
  - Or up-left: center_row - center_col = 48, same ✗
  - Or up-right: center_row + center_col = 66, 33 + 15 = 48 ≠ 66 ✗
So (33, 15) collects (39, 9) and (42, 24) but not (57, 9).
For (57, 9):
- Down-left: center_row + center_col = 66, 46 <= center_row <= 56
- Reachable from (18, 30): center_row = 18 + 3*m, center_col = 30 + 3*n
- center_row + center_col = 48 + 3*(m+n) = 66 → m+n = 6
- And 46 <= 18 + 3*m <= 56 → 28 <= 3*m <= 38 → 9.33 <= m <= 12.67 → m = 10, 11, 12
- If m = 10: n = -4, center = (48, -2) - col out of bounds
- If m = 11: n = -5, center = (51, -5) - col out of bounds
- If m = 12: n = -6, center = (54, -8) - col out of bounds
Hmm, those are out of bounds. Let me try other arm combinations.
For (57, 9) down-right: center_row - center_col = 48
- 18 + 3*m - (30 + 3*n) = 48 → 3*(m-n) = 60 → m-n = 20
- center_row = 18 + 3*m, need 46 <= center_row <= 56 → m = 10, 11, 12
- If m = 10: n = -10, center = (48, 0). Check: 48 - 0 = 48 ✓, k = 57-48 = 9 ✓
  - Position (48, 0): reachable! 10 DOWN + 10 LEFT = 20 actions from (18, 30)
Let me check (48, 0) for all 3 dots:
- (39, 9): 
  - Down-left: 48 + 0 = 48 ≠ 48... wait, center_row + center_col = 48 + 0 = 48. For (39, 9) down-left: center_row + center_col = 48 ✓
  - k = 39 - 48 = -9, negative, not valid
  - Up-left: center_row - center_col = 48, 48 - 0 = 48 ✓
  - k = 48 - 39 = 9, valid ✓. And col: 0 - 9 = -9 ≠ 9. Hmm.
Wait, I need to be more careful. For up-left arm: row = center_row - k, col = center_col - k
- (39, 9): 39 = 48 - k → k = 9, 9 = 0 - k → k = -9. Inconsistent! ✗
Let me redo this more carefully.
For a center at (cr, cc), the X arms cover:
- Up-left: (cr-k, cc-k) for k=1..11
- Up-right: (cr-k, cc+k) for k=1..11
- Down-left: (cr+k, cc-k) for k=1..11
- Down-right: (cr+k, cc+k) for k=1..11
For dot (39, 9) at center (48, 0):
- Up-left: 39 = 48-k, 9 = 0-k → k=9, k=-9. Inconsistent ✗
- Up-right: 39 = 48-k, 9 = 0+k → k=9, k=9. Consistent! k=9, valid ✓
- Down-left: 39 = 48+k, 9 = 0-k → k=-9, k=-9. Negative ✗
- Down-right: 39 = 48+k, 9 = 0+k → k=-9, k=9. Inconsistent ✗
So (39, 9) is on the up-right arm from (48, 0).
For dot (42, 24) at center (48, 0):
- Up-left: 42 = 48-k, 24 = 0-k → k=6, k=-24. Inconsistent ✗
- Up-right: 42 = 48-k, 24 = 0+k → k=6, k=24. Inconsistent ✗
- Down-left: 42 = 48+k, 24 = 0-k → k=-6, k=-24. Negative ✗
- Down-right: 42 = 48+k, 24 = 0+k → k=-6, k=24. Inconsistent ✗
So (42, 24) is NOT on any arm from (48, 0). ✗
For dot (57, 9) at center (48, 0):
- Down-right: 57 = 48+k, 9 = 0+k → k=9, k=9. Consistent! k=9, valid ✓
So (48, 0) collects (39, 9) and (57, 9) but not (42, 24).
Hmm, it seems hard to collect all 3 at once. Let me think about collecting them in 2 positions.
Option 1: Collect (39, 9) and (42, 24) at (33, 15), then (57, 9) separately.
Option 2: Collect (39, 9) and (57, 9) at (48, 0), then (42, 24) separately.
For (33, 15):
From (18, 30): 5 DOWN + 5 LEFT = 10 actions
Then to collect (57, 9): need to find a position that collects (57, 9)
- (57, 9) down-right from (48, 0): center (48, 0)
- From (33, 15): 5 DOWN + 5 LEFT = 10 actions
Total: 20 actions
For (48, 0):
From (18, 30): 10 DOWN + 10 LEFT = 20 actions
Then to collect (42, 24): need to find a position
- (42, 24) down-left: center_row + center_col = 66, 31 <= center_row <= 41
- Reachable: center_row = 18 + 3*m, center_col = 30 + 3*n, m+n = (66-48)/3 = 6
- center_row = 18 + 3*m, 31 <= 18+3m <= 41 → 13 <= 3m <= 23 → m = 5, 6, 7
- m=5: n=1, center = (33, 33). Check: 33+33=66 ✓, k=42-33=9 ✓
  - From (48, 0): 5 UP + 11 RIGHT... 33-48=-15, -15/3=-5. 33-0=33, 33/3=11. So 5 UP + 11 RIGHT = 16 actions.
  - Total: 20 + 16 = 36 actions. That's a lot.
Hmm, let me think more efficiently. Maybe I should collect all 3 orange dots in 2 stops.
Let me find positions that collect 2 dots each:
For (39, 9) and (42, 24):
- (39, 9) up-right: center_row + center_col = 48, center_row >= 40
- (42, 24) down-left: center_row + center_col = 66, center_row <= 41
- Different sums, can't be on same arm type.
- (39, 9) up-right: center_row + center_col = 48
- (42, 24) up-right: center_row + center_col = 66
- Different sums.
- (39, 9) up-right: center_row + center_col = 48
- (42, 24) down-right: center_row - center_col = 18
  - Solving: center_row = 33, center_col = 15. Already found this.
- (39, 9) down-left: center_row + center_col = 48
- (42, 24) up-left: center_row - center_col = 18
  - Solving: center_row = 33, center_col = 15. Same position!
So (33, 15) collects (39, 9) and (42, 24). Let me verify:
- (39, 9) at center (33, 15):
  - Down-left: 39 = 33+k, 9 = 15-k → k=6, k=6. ✓
- (42, 24) at center (33, 15):
  - Down-right: 42 = 33+k, 24 = 15+k → k=9, k=9. ✓
Now for (57, 9):
- (57, 9) down-left: center_row + center_col = 66
- Reachable positions: center_row = 18 + 3*m, center_col = 30 + 3*n
- 18 + 3*m + 30 + 3*n = 66 → m + n = 6
- k = 57 - center_row = 57 - (18 + 3*m) = 39 - 3*m
- Need 1 <= k <= 11: 1 <= 39 - 3*m <= 11 → 28 <= 3*m <= 38 → m = 10, 11, 12
- m=10: n=-4, center = (48, -12). Out of bounds.
- m=11: n=-5, center = (51, -15). Out of bounds.
- m=12: n=-6, center = (54, -18). Out of bounds.
- (57, 9) down-right: center_row - center_col = 48
- 18 + 3*m - (30 + 3*n) = 48 → m - n = 20
- k = 57 - center_row = 57 - (18 + 3*m) = 39 - 3*m
- Need 1 <= k <= 11: m = 10, 11, 12
- m=10: n=-10, center = (48, 0). k = 9. ✓
- m=11: n=-9, center = (51, 3). k = 6. ✓
- m=12: n=-8, center = (54, 6). k = 3. ✓
Let me check (51, 3) for all 3 dots:
- (39, 9) at (51, 3):
  - Up-right: 39 = 51-k, 9 = 3+k → k=12, k=6. Inconsistent ✗
  - Up-left: 39 = 51-k, 9 = 3-k → k=12, k=-6. Inconsistent ✗
  - Down-left: 39 = 51+k → k=-12. Negative ✗
  - Down-right: 39 = 51+k → k=-12. Negative ✗
So (51, 3) doesn't collect (39, 9).
What about (54, 6)?
- (39, 9) at (54, 6):
  - Up-right: 39 = 54-k, 9 = 6+k → k=15, k=3. Inconsistent ✗
  - Up-left: 39 = 54-k, 9 = 6-k → k=15, k=-3. Inconsistent ✗
  - Down-left/right: k negative. ✗
So (54, 6) doesn't collect (39, 9) either.
It seems like (57, 9) is hard to combine with the other dots. Let me try a different approach: collect (42, 24) and (57, 9) together.
For (42, 24) and (57, 9):
- (42, 24) down-left: center_row + center_col = 66
- (57, 9) down-left: center_row + center_col = 66
Same sum! So they can be on the same arm.
- k for (42, 24): k = 42 - center_row
- k for (57, 9): k = 57 - center_row
- Need both 1-11: 31 <= center_row <= 41 and 46 <= center_row <= 56
- Overlap: 46 <= center_row <= 46 → center_row = 46
- center_col = 66 - 46 = 20
- Check: (42, 24) down-left: k = 42-46 = -4. Negative! ✗
Hmm, the k values overlap but in opposite directions. Let me try different arms.
- (42, 24) up-right: center_row + center_col = 66, center_row >= 43
- (57, 9) down-left: center_row + center_col = 66, center_row <= 56
- Same sum, different arm types.
- k for (42, 24) up-right: k = center_row - 42
- k for (57, 9) down-left: k = 57 - center_row
- Need 1 <= center_row - 42 <= 11: 43 <= center_row <= 53
- Need 1 <= 57 - center_row <= 11: 46 <= center_row <= 56
- Overlap: 46 <= center_row <= 53
- center_row = 18 + 3*m: 46 <= 18+3m <= 53 → 28 <= 3m <= 35 → m = 10, 11
- m=10: center_row = 48, center_col = 66-48 = 18. k1 = 48-42 = 6 ✓, k2 = 57-48 = 9 ✓
- m=11: center_row = 51, center_col = 66-51 = 15. k1 = 51-42 = 9 ✓, k2 = 57-51 = 6 ✓
Let me check (48, 18) for all 3 dots:
- (39, 9) at (48, 18):
  - Up-right: 39 = 48-k, 9 = 18+k → k=9, k=-9. Inconsistent ✗
  - Up-left: 39 = 48-k, 9 = 18-k → k=9, k=9. ✓! k=9, valid ✓
- (42, 24) at (48, 18):
  - Up-right: 42 = 48-k, 24 = 18+k → k=6, k=6. ✓! k=6, valid ✓
- (57, 9) at (48, 18):
  - Down-left: 57 = 48+k, 9 = 18-k → k=9, k=9. ✓! k=9, valid ✓
(48, 18) collects ALL 3 orange dots! 
From (18, 30) to (48, 18):
- Row: 48 - 18 = 30, 30/3 = 10 DOWN
- Col: 18 - 30 = -12, -12/3 = 4 LEFT
Total: 14 actions

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model:
- Level 2: Player controls orange diagonal X cross, moves 3 pixels per action
- Player at (18, 30), orange X arms are 11 pixels long in 4 diagonal directions
- 3 orange target dots to collect: (39, 9), (42, 24), (57, 9)
- Found optimal position: (48, 18) collects ALL 3 orange dots simultaneously
  - (39, 9): up-left arm, k=9
  - (42, 24): up-right arm, k=6  
  - (57, 9): down-left arm, k=9
- Path: 10 DOWN + 4 LEFT = 14 actions from (18, 30)

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move to (48, 18) to collect all 3 orange dots
# From (18, 30): 10 DOWN + 4 LEFT = 14 actions
actions = ['DOWN']*10 + ['LEFT']*4
result = action(actions)
print("Result:", result)

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Find player
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"Player at: {n['boundary']}")
        break

# Count remaining orange dots
orange_1px = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'O' and n['pixels'] == 1]
print(f"Orange 1px dots remaining: {len(orange_1px)}")

# Check level completion
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
print(f"Score: {last_action_result.get('score')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
